response: The global food system faces rising pressure as the population is projected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, requiring about 60% more food production. Climate change, water scarcity, aquifer depletion, and soil degradation—affecting 33% of arable land—are reducing agricultural productivity, while agriculture contributes roughly 25% of global greenhouse gas emissions. Food waste worsens the challenge, with about one-third of all food produced lost or wasted. Potential solutions include precision agriculture, vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant crops, regenerative farming, and policy reforms, but they must be scaled while keeping food affordable and accessible.
